_comment: All pixel coordinates are based on a 1920x1080 capture. If your capture resolution differs, set base_width/base_height to match how these were measured (1920x1080) and the tool scales automatically. Tune regions with calibrate mode.
_capture_help: Live capture uses Windows Graphics Capture (like OBS). mode 'monitor' (default) captures the whole monitor Apex is on (auto-detected from the Apex window) - this is the smooth path for fullscreen games. mode 'window' captures just the Apex window (works anywhere but can make a fullscreen game stutter). monitor_index is the fallback monitor if Apex isn't found. throttle_ms caps frame delivery (higher = lighter).
_low_priority_help: Run the tracker at below-normal process priority so Windows favors Apex. Recommended true.
_settle_help: Seconds to wait after the end-screen banner appears before reading it, so placement/stats are fully drawn (prevents blank/partial rows).
_heartbeat_help: Seconds between 'still watching' status lines so you can tell it's alive. Set 0 to disable.
_new_file_each_run_help: false = one running file that accumulates every match (default). true = a new timestamped file each time you start 'watch', e.g. apex_matches_20260604_193512.csv.
_known_names_help: Recurring squadmate gamertags. OCR names are snapped to the closest match here (fixes stray glyphs like WhopperGob->WhopperGot). Add your squad's names.
_resolution_help: The regions below are measured at base_width x base_height (1920x1080) and auto-scale to whatever resolution is captured, which already covers any 16:9 res (1440p, 4K). force_resolution pins a profile key like '2560x1440' (run 'setup' to set it interactively, or pass --res); leave it empty to auto-detect each run. profiles holds per-resolution region sets for cases where pure scaling is slightly off - each profile carries its own base_width/base_height plus header/columns/rows/detect blocks measured at that native resolution, and is used as-is (no scaling) when its key matches. See CALIBRATION.md.

detect._comment: Banner region, used ONLY to recognize the end-of-match summary screen (win = gold CHAMPIONS, any other placement = red SQUAD ELIMINATED). The banner text itself is not stored. A cheap gold/red color check gates a confirming OCR, so OCR does not run during gameplay.
